modes.name: Pyrolysis
modes.description: Heats the sample in stages. Volatiles evaporate at different temperature thresholds — higher temperatures release more tightly-bound molecules.
modes.hint: Best for organics, volatiles, and water-bound compounds. Use mouse Y to control oven temperature.

modes.name: Wet Chemistry
modes.description: Dissolves a portion of the sample in chemical reagent, then runs the solution through the mass spectrometer. Requires subsurface ice as solvent base.
modes.hint: Best for mineral ID and elemental ratios. Drag sliders to balance reagent mix. Requires 1 ice per run.

modes.name: Isotope Analysis
modes.description: Fires a laser through released gases, measuring absorption at specific wavelengths. Identifies isotope ratios that fingerprint biological vs geological origin.
modes.hint: Best on samples with interesting pyrolysis/wet-chem results. Tune laser with mouse X — lock onto peaks, avoid artifacts.

discoveries.name: Volcanic glass
discoveries.description: Amorphous silicate formed during rapid lava cooling. Common on volcanic plains.

discoveries.name: Iron(III) oxide
discoveries.description: Fe₂O₃ — the compound that makes Mars red. Abundant everywhere.

discoveries.name: Forsterite
discoveries.description: Mg₂SiO₄ crystal. Deep mantle mineral that reached the surface through volcanic uplift.

discoveries.name: Calcium sulfate
discoveries.description: Anhydrite/gypsum. Precipitated from ancient evaporating water bodies.

discoveries.name: Montmorillonite clay
discoveries.description: Smectite clay formed by chemical weathering in the presence of water.

discoveries.name: Kamacite
discoveries.description: Iron-nickel alloy. The backbone of iron meteorites. Not from Mars.

discoveries.name: Adsorbed water
discoveries.description: Trace water molecules clinging to mineral surfaces. Mars isn't completely dry.

discoveries.name: Geological carbon
discoveries.description: Standard C12/C13 ratio. Volcanic outgassing carbon — no biological origin.

discoveries.name: Manganese oxide
discoveries.description: MnO₂ — on Earth, often produced by microbial activity. On Mars... unclear.

discoveries.name: Serpentinization evidence
discoveries.description: Olivine + water → serpentine + hydrogen. The hydrogen could feed microbial life.

discoveries.name: Chlorinated compounds
discoveries.description: Chloromethane and dichloromethane. Could be contamination. Could be Martian. Controversial.

discoveries.name: Organic volatiles
discoveries.description: Low-temperature carbon compounds released during gentle heating. The first hint.

discoveries.name: Platinum group metals
discoveries.description: Iridium, platinum, osmium. Concentrated in meteorites.

discoveries.name: Ancient water signature
discoveries.description: D/H ratio indicates this rock interacted with liquid water billions of years ago.

discoveries.name: Phyllosilicate
discoveries.description: Layered clay mineral — forms only in sustained liquid water. Habitable environment indicator.

discoveries.name: Perchlorate chemistry
discoveries.description: ClO₄⁻ salts. Toxic to humans but fascinating chemistry.

discoveries.name: Thiophenic compounds
discoveries.description: Sulfur-bearing organics. Found by the real Curiosity at Gale Crater. Consistent with ancient biology.

discoveries.name: Nickel anomaly
discoveries.description: Nickel concentration far above Martian background. Meteoritic input to the sediment?

discoveries.name: Ancient brine chemistry
discoveries.description: Halite and perchlorate ratios that reconstruct the salinity of a vanished Martian lake.

discoveries.name: Presolar grains
discoveries.description: Mineral grains older than the solar system. Formed in a dying star before our Sun existed.

discoveries.name: Biological sulfur ratio
discoveries.description: S32/S34 ratio showing mass-independent fractionation — a potential biosignature.

discoveries.name: Biosignature candidate
discoveries.description: C12-enriched carbon. Biology preferentially uses lighter isotopes. This is the signal.

discoveries.name: Deep mantle signature
discoveries.description: Noble gas ratios from deep within Mars. A window into the planet's interior.

discoveries.name: Widmanstätten pattern
discoveries.description: Nickel-iron crystal structure that takes millions of years to form. This rock is ancient.

discoveries.name: Aromatic organics
discoveries.description: Benzene, toluene, and chlorobenzene. Complex carbon chemistry in ancient lakebed sediment. The headline discovery.

discoveries.name: Confirmed biosignature
discoveries.description: C12/C13 ratio combined with organic compounds produces a statistically significant biological signature. This is the one.

discoveries.name: Lipid biomarker
discoveries.description: Long-chain fatty acids preserved in clay matrix. On Earth, these are always biological in origin.

discoveries.name: Extraterrestrial organic
discoveries.description: Amino acids and nucleobases embedded in the meteorite matrix. Life's building blocks, delivered from space.

discoveries.name: Extrasolar isotope
discoveries.description: Isotope ratios that don't match any known solar system source. This grain came from another star.

discoveries.name: Chlorinated organic
discoveries.description: Complex chlorinated hydrocarbon in evaporite deposit. Not contamination. Martian.
